inputs.text: A formal engineering blueprint / technical patent diagram of a plate of spaghetti and meatballs, drawn as precise white and pale-cyan line work on a deep blueprint-blue background. The pasta is rendered as an exploded technical schematic: individual strands of spaghetti labeled with part numbers and leader lines, cross-sectional cutaway views of a meatball showing concentric internal layers with callout annotations, and a fork mid-twirl diagrammed with curved motion arrows and angle-of-rotation degrees marked in dotted arcs. Dimension lines with arrowheads measure the plate's diameter and the noodle gauge, complete with tolerance notations. A neat title block in the lower-right corner reads like a patent header with grid coordinates, "FIG. 1," and faux revision numbers. Sauce coverage shown as a hatched fill pattern with a density legend. Top-down orthographic projection plus a small isometric inset in the corner. Everything is monochromatic blueprint cyan-on-blue, crisp uniform line weights, faint grid paper texture underneath, slight aging and fold creases on the paper. Flat, even technical-document lighting with no shadows, as if scanned. Shot as a high-resolution flatbed scan, fine paper grain, the clean reproduction of archival drafting film. Precise, deadpan, wonderfully over-engineered.
